commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be lacking unto them. Paul urges that they not wait for those who are needy to come to them but that they seek out those who need their assistance. Thus the considerate man shows his concern, and with great zeal he will perform his duty. For in doing good actions, it is not those who receive the kindness who are benefitted, so much as those who do the kindness that make gain and profit, for it gives them confidence toward God.
